{{Short description="Scientific study of the origin, evolution, and ultimate fate of the universe}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Cosmology
| image = CMB_Timeline75.jpg
| caption = History of the [[universe]] from the [[Big Bang]] to the present.
| topics = [[General relativity]], [[Universe]], [[Big Bang]], [[Dark matter]], [[Dark energy]]
}}

'''Cosmology''' is a branch of [[astronomy]] concerned with the study of the origin, evolution, and ultimate fate of the [[universe]]. '''Physical cosmology''' is the scientific study of the universe's origin, large-scale structures and dynamics, and its ultimate fate, using the laws of [[physics]].

== Big Bang model ==
{{Main|Big Bang}}
The prevailing cosmological model is the [[Lambda-CDM model]] (ΛCDM), which describes a universe that began approximately 13.8 billion years ago in a hot, dense state (the [[Big Bang]]) and has been expanding ever since.

== Key observational pillars ==

=== Hubble expansion ===
[[Edwin Hubble]] discovered that galaxies recede from us at speeds proportional to their distance:
:<math>v = H_0 d</math>
where <math>H_0 \approx 70\ \text{km/s/Mpc}</math> is the [[Hubble constant]].

=== Cosmic microwave background ===
{{Main|Cosmic microwave background}}
Thermal radiation left over from ~380,000 years after the Big Bang, at temperature <math>T = 2.725\ \text{K}</math>. Discovered by [[Arno Penzias]] and [[Robert Wilson]] in 1965.

=== Big Bang nucleosynthesis ===
Primordial production of light elements (<math>^1\text{H}, ^4\text{He}, ^2\text{D}, ^7\text{Li}</math>) occurred in the first ~3 minutes.

=== Large-scale structure ===
The universe exhibits a web-like structure of galaxies, clusters, and filaments.

== Composition of the universe ==
The ΛCDM model indicates:
* ~68% [[dark energy]] ([[cosmological constant]] <math>\Lambda</math>)
* ~27% [[dark matter]]
* ~5% ordinary [[baryon]]ic matter

== Friedmann equations ==
{{Main|Friedmann equations}}
Derived from the [[Einstein field equations]] for a homogeneous, isotropic universe:

:<math>H^2 = \left(\frac{\dot{a}}{a}\right)^2 = \frac{8\pi G}{3}\rho - \frac{kc^2}{a^2} + \frac{\Lambda c^2}{3}</math>
:<math>\frac{\ddot{a}}{a} = -\frac{4\pi G}{3}\left(\rho + \frac{3p}{c^2}\right) + \frac{\Lambda c^2}{3}</math>

== Open questions ==
* Nature of [[dark matter]] and [[dark energy]]
* [[Cosmic inflation]] — what drove the early exponential expansion?
* [[Baryon asymmetry]] — why matter over antimatter?
* Ultimate fate: [[Big Freeze]], [[Big Rip]], or [[Big Crunch]]?

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Big Bang]]
* [[Dark energy]]
* [[Dark matter]]
* [[General relativity]]
* [[Universe]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Cosmology| ]]
[[Category:Astrophysics]]
[[Category:General relativity]]
